definitions.text: Extending, reaching or positioned far from a point of reference, especially downwards. Extending far down from the top, or surface, to the bottom, literally or figuratively.
examples: We hiked into a deep valley between tall mountains.

definitions.text: Extending far down from the top, or surface, to the bottom, literally or figuratively.
examples: We hiked into a deep valley between tall mountains.

definitions.text: Positioned far from the surface or other reference point, especially down through something or into something.
examples: Diving down to deep wrecks can be dangerous.
I can't get the bullet out – it's too deep.

definitions.text: Far, especially far down through something or into something, physically or figuratively.
examples: The ogre lived in a cave deep underground.
We ventured deep into the forest.

definitions.text: In a profound, not superficial, manner.
The deep part of a lake, sea, etc.
A deep hole or pit, a water well; an abyss.
To overthink; to treat as being deeper (“more profound, significant”) than in reality.
examples: ― Ugh, why are these road markings so awfully arranged?― Quit deeping it bro, just drive, innit.

definitions.text: To think about, especially deeply (“profoundly”); to consider.
